message:

 {"id":"bd-2vr05.10.1","title":"task(conformance): implement VT support-matrix fixture runner","description":"Executable fixture runner tied directly to declared VT support matrix claims.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Behavior in this task is deterministic for fixed inputs and capability profile.
- Required unit and/or E2E tests are added for the scoped behavior.
- Scope is complete only when dependencies remain satisfied and no regressions are introduced.

## Comprehensive Validation Hooks
- This task must add or update focused unit tests for core logic touched.
- This task must link to at least one end-to-end script path that validates integrated behavior.
- Validation output must include structured logs detailed enough for deterministic postmortem analysis.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Comprehensive unit tests are implemented for the scoped subsystem, including edge cases and regression vectors.
- Deterministic end-to-end scripts are implemented for the scoped workflows and runnable in CI.
- Test execution emits detailed structured logs (JSONL) including timestamps, correlation IDs, and failure context.
- At least one deliberate failure injection scenario is covered and produces actionable diagnostics.","notes":"Execution Notes
- Conformance should directly track declared support-matrix claims.
- Differential harness should normalize expected differences and classify mismatches clearly.
- Fuzzing must produce minimized repro artifacts suitable for deterministic regression tests.
- CI gates from this track are intended to be release-blocking, not advisory.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:54.808771543Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","updated_at":"2026-02-24T07:43:47.396814255Z","closed_at":"2026-02-24T07:43:47.396795821Z","close_reason":"Completed VT support-matrix fixture runner + E2E + structured logs + failure injection","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","frankenterm","vt"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.10.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-2vr05.11","title":"feature(frankenterm-js): security posture, bounded resources, and operational observability","description":"Background
- Remote terminal mode introduces meaningful security and reliability risks.
- Production operations need deterministic traces and bounded-resource behavior.

Goal
- Define secure defaults and observability foundations for safe remote deployment.

In Scope
- Threat model for attach/embedding architecture.
- Link/clipboard policy hardening.
- Queue and memory cap enforcement.
- Deterministic trace and replay capabilities.
- Operational runbooks for incident handling.

Out of Scope
- Security assumptions left implicit.

Why This Matters
- Essential for production safety and maintainability.

Acceptance Criteria
- Threat model maps to concrete implementation tasks.
- Overload behavior is bounded and test-backed.
- Incident replay and runbooks support effective triage.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Feature behavior is validated by comprehensive unit tests that cover core invariants and edge cases.
- Feature behavior is validated by end-to-end test scripts that exercise realistic user workflows.
- E2E runs emit detailed structured logs (JSONL or equivalent) sufficient for deterministic failure triage and replay.
- CI gates for this feature fail on regression in correctness, determinism, or declared standards scope.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:37.820444865Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","updated_at":"2026-02-11T03:03:15.420385236Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankenterm","ops","reliability","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.11","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.11","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vr05.11.1","title":"task(security): author threat model for remote attach and embedding","description":"Trust boundaries, attack scenarios, and mitigation mapping for remote/browser architecture.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Behavior in this task is deterministic for fixed inputs and capability profile.
- Required unit and/or E2E tests are added for the scoped behavior.
- Scope is complete only when dependencies remain satisfied and no regressions are introduced.

## Comprehensive Validation Hooks
- This task must add or update focused unit tests for core logic touched.
- This task must link to at least one end-to-end script path that validates integrated behavior.
- Validation output must include structured logs detailed enough for deterministic postmortem analysis.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Threat model maps to concrete mitigations and open risks.
- Policy defaults for links/clipboard are safe and explicit.
- Queue/memory caps are enforced under adversarial load.
- Trace/replay and runbooks support incident triage workflows.

## Mandatory Cross-Cut Quality Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for the implementation surface and edge-case regressions touched by this task.
- Deterministic end-to-end scripts are required for workflows materially affected by this task, and must run in CI.
- Validation output must include detailed structured logs (JSONL) with timestamps, correlation IDs, and failure context sufficient for deterministic replay and triage.","notes":"Execution Notes
- Security defaults should assume hostile or malformed remote traffic.
- Policy controls (links, clipboard) must have safe defaults and explicit override points.
- Resource caps should be hard limits with predictable overload behavior.
- Trace/replay outputs should support post-incident root-cause workflows.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:57.196609809Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","updated_at":"2026-02-15T01:30:16.804117362Z","closed_at":"2026-02-15T01:30:16.804098888Z","close_reason":"Completed: threat model authored, secure defaults enforced, tests added","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankenterm","security","threat-model"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.11.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.11","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

@@ -30,6 +30,9 @@ path = "../crates/ftui-render"
 [dependencies.ftui-widgets]
 path = "../crates/ftui-widgets"
 
+[dependencies.ftui-pty]
+path = "../crates/ftui-pty"
+
 [[bin]]
 name = "fuzz_input_parser"
 path = "fuzz_targets/fuzz_input_parser.rs"
@@ -51,9 +54,16 @@ test = false
 doc = false
 bench = false
 
-## NOTE: fuzz_vt_parser and fuzz_grid_mutations removed — frankenterm-core crate
-## was removed from the workspace. These targets need to be rewritten against
-## the current parser/grid API if/when it's available again.
+[[bin]]
+name = "fuzz_vt_parser"
+path = "fuzz_targets/fuzz_vt_parser.rs"
+test = false
+doc = false
+bench = false
+
+## NOTE: fuzz_grid_mutations was removed from the active target set because the
+## old frankenterm-core grid/cursor API is no longer vendored in this workspace.
+## Rewrite it against the current grid API before re-adding it here.
 
 [[bin]]
 name = "fuzz_text_cluster_map"

@@ -9,6 +9,7 @@ This directory contains cargo-fuzz targets for FrankenTUI.
 | `fuzz_input_parser` | ftui-core | Raw byte input parser (no-panic) |
 | `fuzz_input_parser_structured` | ftui-core | Structured input events via Arbitrary |
 | `fuzz_input_parser_long_seq` | ftui-core | Long input sequences |
+| `fuzz_vt_parser` | ftui-pty | Virtual terminal state machine, including whole-stream vs chunked feeds |
 | `fuzz_text_cluster_map` | ftui-text | Grapheme cluster mapping |
 | `fuzz_text_shaped_layout` | ftui-text | Shaped text layout pipeline |
 | `fuzz_text_wrap` | ftui-text | Text wrapping (Word/Char/WordChar/Optimal) |
@@ -24,6 +25,7 @@ This directory contains cargo-fuzz targets for FrankenTUI.
 cargo +nightly fuzz run fuzz_layout_constraints -- -max_len=256
 cargo +nightly fuzz run fuzz_widget_render -- -max_len=512
 cargo +nightly fuzz run fuzz_text_wrap -- -max_len=512
+cargo +nightly fuzz run fuzz_vt_parser -- -max_len=4096
 
 # Run all targets briefly (smoke test)
 for target in $(cargo +nightly fuzz list 2>/dev/null); do
@@ -36,4 +38,10 @@ done
 
 - `fuzz/target/` contains build artifacts (ignored by git).
 - `fuzz/artifacts/` contains crash reproducers (ignored by git).
-- `fuzz_vt_parser` and `fuzz_grid_mutations` were removed (frankenterm-core crate no longer exists).
+- `scripts/fuzz_campaign_e2e.sh` emits JSONL records with a run ID,
+  correlation IDs, target log paths, artifact paths, and replay commands.
+- Set `FUZZ_INJECT_CRASH_TARGET=<target>` and
+  `FUZZ_ARTIFACT_ROOT=/tmp/frankentui-fuzz-artifacts` to validate the deliberate
+  failure-injection path without polluting the repository.
+- `fuzz_grid_mutations` remains inactive because it targets the removed
+  frankenterm-core grid/cursor API.

@@ -14,6 +14,10 @@ set -euo pipefail
 #   FUZZ_JOBS            — parallel jobs per target (default: 1)
 #   LOG_DIR              — output directory for logs and JSONL
 #   NIGHTLY_TOOLCHAIN    — nightly toolchain name (default: nightly)
+#   RUN_ID               — deterministic run id override
+#   FUZZ_TARGET_FILTER   — optional exact target name for focused smoke runs
+#   FUZZ_ARTIFACT_ROOT   — crash artifact root (default: fuzz/artifacts)
+#   FUZZ_INJECT_CRASH_TARGET — target name for deliberate artifact failure injection
 #
 # Usage:
 #   ./scripts/fuzz_campaign_e2e.sh
@@ -27,8 +31,14 @@ FUZZ_DURATION_SECS="${FUZZ_DURATION_SECS:-30}"
 FUZZ_MAX_LEN="${FUZZ_MAX_LEN:-4096}"
 FUZZ_JOBS="${FUZZ_JOBS:-1}"
 NIGHTLY_TOOLCHAIN="${NIGHTLY_TOOLCHAIN:-nightly}"
+FUZZ_TARGET_FILTER="${FUZZ_TARGET_FILTER:-}"
 LOG_DIR="${LOG_DIR:-/tmp/fuzz_campaign_e2e_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S)}"
 LOG_JSONL="$LOG_DIR/fuzz_campaign_e2e.jsonl"
+RUN_ID="${RUN_ID:-fuzz-campaign-$(date -u +%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ)-$$}"
+RUN_ID="${RUN_ID//[^A-Za-z0-9_.:-]/_}"
+FUZZ_ARTIFACT_ROOT="${FUZZ_ARTIFACT_ROOT:-$FUZZ_DIR/artifacts}"
+FUZZ_INJECT_CRASH_TARGET="${FUZZ_INJECT_CRASH_TARGET:-}"
+CORRELATION_SEQ=0
 mkdir -p "$LOG_DIR"
 
 # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
@@ -58,8 +68,11 @@ print(json.dumps(data, sort_keys=True))
 emit_event() {
     local event="$1"
     shift
+    CORRELATION_SEQ=$((CORRELATION_SEQ + 1))
+    local correlation_id
+    correlation_id="$(printf '%s-corr-%04d' "$RUN_ID" "$CORRELATION_SEQ")"
     local extra="$*"
-    log_json "{\"event\":\"$event\"$extra}"
+    log_json "{\"event\":\"$event\",\"run_id\":\"$RUN_ID\",\"correlation_id\":\"$correlation_id\"$extra}"
 }
 
 # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
@@ -74,15 +87,29 @@ while IFS= read -r line; do
     fi
 done < <(grep '^name = "fuzz_' "$FUZZ_DIR/Cargo.toml")
 
+if [[ -n "$FUZZ_TARGET_FILTER" ]]; then
+    FILTERED_TARGETS=()
+    for target in "${FUZZ_TARGETS[@]}"; do
+        if [[ "$target" == "$FUZZ_TARGET_FILTER" ]]; then
+            FILTERED_TARGETS+=("$target")
+        fi
+    done
+    FUZZ_TARGETS=("${FILTERED_TARGETS[@]}")
+fi
+
 if [[ ${#FUZZ_TARGETS[@]} -eq 0 ]]; then
     echo "ERROR: No fuzz targets found in $FUZZ_DIR/Cargo.toml"
+    if [[ -n "$FUZZ_TARGET_FILTER" ]]; then
+        echo "Filter did not match any declared target: $FUZZ_TARGET_FILTER"
+    fi
     exit 1
 fi
 
 echo "=== Fuzz Campaign E2E ==="
 echo "Targets: ${#FUZZ_TARGETS[@]}"
 echo "Duration per target: ${FUZZ_DURATION_SECS}s"
 echo "Max input length: ${FUZZ_MAX_LEN}"
+echo "Run ID: $RUN_ID"
 echo "Log directory: $LOG_DIR"
 echo ""
 
@@ -96,8 +123,11 @@ emit_event "env" \
     ",\"max_len\":$FUZZ_MAX_LEN" \
     ",\"jobs\":$FUZZ_JOBS" \
     ",\"nightly_toolchain\":\"$NIGHTLY_TOOLCHAIN\"" \
+    ",\"target_filter\":\"$FUZZ_TARGET_FILTER\"" \
     ",\"git_commit\":\"$(git -C "$PROJECT_ROOT" rev-parse --short HEAD 2>/dev/null || echo unknown)\"" \
     ",\"platform\":\"$(uname -s)\"" \
+    ",\"artifact_root\":\"$FUZZ_ARTIFACT_ROOT\"" \
+    ",\"failure_injection_target\":\"$FUZZ_INJECT_CRASH_TARGET\"" \
     ",\"targets\":$(printf '%s\n' "${FUZZ_TARGETS[@]}" | python3 -c 'import json,sys; print(json.dumps([l.strip() for l in sys.stdin]))')"
 
 # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
@@ -153,8 +183,16 @@ for target in "${FUZZ_TARGETS[@]}"; do
     TARGET_STATUS="pass"
     TARGET_CRASHES=0
     TARGET_RUNS=0
+    TARGET_ARTIFACT_DIR="$FUZZ_ARTIFACT_ROOT/$target"
+    TARGET_REPLAY_CMD="cargo +$NIGHTLY_TOOLCHAIN fuzz run $target --fuzz-dir $FUZZ_DIR"
+    mkdir -p "$TARGET_ARTIFACT_DIR"
 
-    emit_event "target_start" ",\"target\":\"$target\",\"duration_secs\":$FUZZ_DURATION_SECS"
+    emit_event "target_start" \
+        ",\"target\":\"$target\"" \
+        ",\"duration_secs\":$FUZZ_DURATION_SECS" \
+        ",\"target_log\":\"$TARGET_LOG\"" \
+        ",\"artifact_dir\":\"$TARGET_ARTIFACT_DIR\"" \
+        ",\"replay_command\":\"$TARGET_REPLAY_CMD\""
 
     # Run cargo-fuzz with timeout.
     # libfuzzer returns 0 on clean exit, 77 on timeout (expected), non-zero on crash.
@@ -166,23 +204,34 @@ for target in "${FUZZ_TARGETS[@]}"; do
         -max_total_time="$FUZZ_DURATION_SECS" \
         -jobs="$FUZZ_JOBS" \
         -print_final_stats=1 \
+        -artifact_prefix="$TARGET_ARTIFACT_DIR/" \
         > "$TARGET_LOG" 2>&1
     FUZZ_EXIT=$?
     set -e
